markets.question: Will there be no head of state of Venezuela end of 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the individual who officially holds the position of the head of state of Venezuela on Dec 31, 2026 at 12 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, "officially holds" refers to the individual that was formally appointed, confirmed (if confirmation is required), and sworn in as the head of state of Venezuela or otherwise confirmed by official government information as being the head of state of Venezuela. 

If the Venezuelan government does not clearly state who is the head of state, the market will resolve to the individual who is listed as the Head of State by the UN (see: https://www.un.org/dgacm/en/content/protocol/hshgnfa).  

In the event that more than one official head of state is listed by the Venezuelan  government, this market will resolve to the individual who is listed as having primary status. If no distinction is made, the market will resolve to the individual who first assumed the position. 

If no individual holds the position this market will resolve to “No Head of State”. 

The following do NOT constitute "officially holding" the role: nominated, announced, or designated as the head of state of Venezuela but appointment not yet effective; appointed with an effective date after Dec 31, 2026; previously served as the head of state of Venezuela but term has expired, resigned, or been terminated before Dec 31, 2026; serving as the head of state of Venezuela in a consultant, contractor, or unofficial capacity; named as successor or heir apparent to the head of state of Venezuela but not yet appointed; or holding a different role with oversight of the head of state of Venezuela but not the specified position itself.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the UN recognized government of Venezuela. If the Government of Venezuela does not clearly state who is the head of state, information from the UN and a consensus of credible reporting may be used. 

Note: this market is mutually exclusive.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices Nicolás Maduro as the frontrunner at 61.7% to remain Venezuela's head of state by December 31, 2026, reflecting his continued UN recognition despite a January 3 U.S. military capture and ongoing narcoterrorism prosecution in New York federal court. A recent ruling allowing access to Venezuelan government funds for his defense—coupled with a June 30 hearing scheduled by Judge Alvin Hellerstein—has driven this shift, signaling procedural legitimacy tied to his prior presidency. Delcy Rodríguez, sworn in as acting president on January 5 and retaining power beyond her expired 90-day term amid military reshuffles and purges of Maduro allies, holds 23.5%. Opposition leader María Corina Machado lags at 6.5%, hampered by reports of potential chavismo-opposition pacts stalling transition talks.

markets.question: Will Argentina’s annual inflation in 2026 be between 30% and 34.9%?
markets.description:
This is a market about Argentinian inflation over the 12-month period ending December 2026, before seasonal adjustment, as reported by the National Institute of Statistics and Census (INDEC) of Argentina.

This market will resolve according to the percentage change in the Consumer Price Index (CPI / IPC) over the 12-month period ending in December 2026 (Variación % interanual Total nacional) according to the monthly INDEC report.

The resolution source for this market will be the INDEC Consumer Price Index report released for December 2026 (https://www.indec.gob.ar/), expected to be released in January 2027. Resolution of this market will take place upon release of the aforementioned data. If no data for the specified month is released by the date the next month's data is scheduled to be released, this market will resolve based on data from the last available month.

You can find this report by clicking on the “Precios al consumidor” option on the home page of https://www.indec.gob.ar/, and searching the pdf for the figure under “Variación % interanual Total nacional”.

Note: the resolution source for this market will be the official monthly INDEC CPI (IPC) news release which reports inflation over 12 month periods to only one decimal point (e.g. 33.6%). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices Argentina's 2026 annual inflation in a tight race between the 25-29.9% (32%) and 30-34.9% (31.5%) bins, reflecting March 2026 CPI data showing 3.4% monthly inflation—the highest year-to-date—pushing year-over-year rates to 32.6% amid rising transportation and education costs. President Milei's fiscal austerity has slowed annual inflation from 2025 peaks above 200%, but reacceleration risks from seasonal factors and looser monetary signals have lifted central bank surveys to 29.1% full-year expectations and IMF forecasts to 30.5%. Key differentiators include April CPI (due mid-May) and sustained monthly deceleration below 3%; persistent 3%+ prints favor higher bins, while sub-2.5% trajectory supports the lower range.

markets.question: Will the US acquire part of Greenland in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the United States acquires control of any land territory that is part of Greenland by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Only the transfer of sovereignty, or the acquisition of primary or exclusive jurisdiction or control qualifies.

1. Transfer of Sovereignty: This will qualify if a binding agreement or legal instrument results in a defined area of Greenland coming under the formal sovereignty of the U.S. (e.g., incorporated as a U.S. state, territory, possession, or other U.S. political classification), even if the effective date occurs after the market deadline.

2. Acquisition of Primary or Exclusive Jurisdiction or Control: This will qualify if a binding agreement or legal instrument establishes a defined area in Greenland in which the U.S. has primary or exclusive jurisdiction or control over the territory, such that the ordinary legal authority of Denmark and Greenland do not apply,except by U.S. permission. Such agreements or instruments will qualify even if the effective date occurs after the market deadline. 

3. Use of Force: If the U.S. acquires primary or exclusive jurisdiction or control over a defined area of Greenland through force, this will also qualify. 

An announcement will qualify only if it is accompanied by or consists of a binding agreement or legal instrument  (e.g., enacted legislation, a signed treaty, the signed text of an agreement, or an executive action implementing such an agreement) that unambiguously creates a transfer of sovereignty, or primary or exclusive jurisdiction or control,  even if this transfer or acquisition takes effect after the market deadline. 

Non-binding statements, negotiations, proposals, frameworks, or MOUs will not alone qualify. Basing rights, access agreements, SOFA-type arrangements, COFA-type arrangements, commercial concessions, or other permissions to use land (including leases) will not alone qualify. Any qualifying U.S. jurisdiction or control  in Greenland that existed at market creation will not count as new qualifying control.

Examples of qualifying events include but are not limited to treaty or piece of legislation that makes any portion of Greenland a U.S. territory or possession, even if the handover date for such territory or possession is later); or, a Guantánamo-style arrangement establishing a defined zone in Greenland under exclusive or primary U.S. jurisdiction and control, where Denmark and Greenland’s ordinary legal authority does not apply except by U.S. permission.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the governments of the United States, Denmark, and Greenland; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices "No" at 86.5% for US acquisition of any part of Greenland in 2026, reflecting firm Danish and Greenlandic rejections amid legal and diplomatic barriers. President Trump's January 2026 Davos push for immediate negotiations and reports of a potential $500–700 billion US offer generated brief speculation, but Denmark reiterated Greenland is "not for sale," citing sovereignty and NATO alliance strains. No formal talks or agreements have advanced since, with international law prohibiting unilateral annexation. Absent a seismic shift like Danish consent or Greenland independence referendum, structural obstacles—Greenland's autonomous status under Denmark and geopolitical fallout—sustain high odds against any transfer by year-end.

markets.question: Will Joe Flacco start Week 1 for the Chiefs in 2026?

markets.question: Will Elon Musk post 1160-1199 tweets in May 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X during the month of May 2026.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

markets.question: Will Apple (AAPL) hit (LOW) $272 Week of May 4 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during the week of May 4 2026, any 1-minute candle for Apple (AAPL) has a final "Low" price equal to or below the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange on which the listed security trades (typically 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM ET) will be considered. Prices occurring during pre-market or after-hours trading will not qualify.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Pyth after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Apple (AAPL) "Low" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.AAPL%2FUSD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter. Any timestamp within the listed market time frame may be used to view the relevant candle data (e.g., https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.AAPL%2FUSD?t=1773432000)

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily low price published by the primary exchange on which the listed security trades will be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Miami be 79°F or below on May 6?

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Miami Intl Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/us/fl/miami/KMIA.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Fahrenheit (eg, 21°F). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

markets.question: Will there be between 10 and 20 average daily transits of the Strait of Hormuz on April 30?

Transit calls include container, dry bulk, roll-on/roll-off, general cargo, and tanker ships. Ships not reported by IMF Portwatch will not be considered.

markets.question: Will US withdraw from NATO before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the United States formally initiates a withdrawal from NATO or provides an official notice of denunciation to NATO by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A notice of denunciation refers to the submission of a notice of withdrawal as per Article 13 of the North Atlantic Treaty.

Any action meeting these criteria will qualify for a “Yes” resolution regardless of if its implementation is immediately halted or delayed by judicial or other actions.

The U.S.'s exit from NATO’s integrated military command structure will not be sufficient to resolve this market to "Yes". 

The resolution source will be official information from the US government and NATO, however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

This market resolves according to (1) the official toss result and (2) the finalized match result as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.
The outcome corresponding to Philippines will be considered correct if  Philippines is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
The outcome corresponding to Indonesia will be considered correct if Indonesia is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
In all other cases - including where the toss and match winners differ, or where no match winner is recorded - the market will resolve to Neither.
DLS/DRS adjustments, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, Super Over, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a match winner are treated as ordinary wins. If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the tiebreak winner will be considered the match winner for this market. If the match ends tied with no tiebreak used or available, no team will be considered to have won the match and the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without an official match winner being declared, the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and will then resolve as described above.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the MD-06 House seat?

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Lucknow be 40°C on May 7?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Chaudhary Charan Singh Intl Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 7 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Chaudhary Charan Singh Intl Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/in/lucknow/VILK.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent thundery showers and cloud cover have driven unseasonably low temperatures in Lucknow, with the India Meteorological Department (IMD) recording a May 4 maximum of 29°C—10.4°C below normal—amid 14.8 mm rainfall, suppressing daytime heating. IMD forecasts partly cloudy skies with further rain/thundershowers likely through May 7 across Uttar Pradesh, including Lucknow, where May 5 highs are expected around 31°C before a gradual 4-6°C rise, potentially reaching 36-38°C per extended range outlooks for central plains. Trader consensus favors ≤35°C at 36% implied probability, reflecting persistent atmospheric instability capping peaks below typical May norms of 39-42°C, though haze and clearing could enable rebound; monitor IMD's May 5 evening bulletin for updated model consensus.

eventMetadata.context_description: Jean-Luc Mélenchon, leader of hard-left La France Insoumise, announced on May 4, 2026, his fourth bid for the April 2027 presidential election, defying prior vows to step aside and joining a crowded field to succeed term-limited Emmanuel Macron. markets.question: Will the Democratic Party win the NM-01 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the NM-01 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

markets.question: Will the Fed’s lower bound reach 0.5% or lower before 2027?
markets.description:
The FED interest rates are defined in this market by the lower or the upper bound of the target federal funds range. The decisions on the target federal fund range are made by the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the lower or the upper bound of the target federal funds rate reaches the specified level at any point by December 31, 2026, 12:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

Emergency rate cuts and hikes outside the regularly scheduled meetings will be considered.

The resolution source for this market is the official website of the Federal Reserve at:
https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm.

This market may resolve as soon as the relevant data showing the reached level is published.

Elena Rybakina follows at 19.4% after her Australian Open victory over Sabalenka and Stuttgart clay title, showcasing versatile hard-court prowess as World No. 2. Iga Swiatek lags at 11.5% due to hard-court inconsistencies—early Indian Wells quarterfinal exit and Miami second-round upset—exacerbated by her recent Madrid retirement from illness. The wide-open field highlights depth, with Coco Gauff's consistency and risers like Xiyu Wang drawing value on recent momentum despite lower rankings.

markets.question: Spread: IK Start (-1.5)

Key absences include Viking goalkeepers Arild Østbø and Edvin Austbø plus defender Martin Ove Roseth, while Start misses Fredrik Pålerud, Johan Meyer, and Steve Mvoué; home advantage and superior squad depth tilt trader consensus toward the hosts amid Start's defensive woes.

In February 2026, a House Oversight Committee deposition saw Wexner, Epstein's longtime financial patron, deny knowledge of the sex offender's crimes, claiming he was duped; lawmakers questioned his credibility but took no further prosecutorial steps. A civil lawsuit filed by Epstein survivors in early April alleges Wexner enabled the abuse through funding and property, yet distinguishes from criminal proceedings. With no active federal investigation or scheduled hearings announced, and Wexner uncharged since Epstein's 2019 death, traders price significant barriers to near-term prosecution.

markets.question: Will Trump and Putin meet next in another EU country?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the location of the next meeting between Donald Trump and Vladimir Putin between September 30 and June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve to "No meeting by June 30" if no qualifying meeting occurs during this market's timeframe.

A meeting is defined as any encounter where Putin and Trump are all present and interact with each other in person.

An exchange of words, handshake, direct conversation, or other clear personal interaction between the named individuals will qualify as a meeting. Merely standing in proximity, making eye contact, or being present in the same room or event without direct interaction will not qualify.

For this market, Gulf states are defined as the six members of the Gulf Cooperation Council (Bahrain, Kuwait, Oman, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, United Arab Emirates).

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

Reports of a U.S. invitation for Putin to the December G20 in Miami fall after the deadline. A sudden breakthrough in bilateral negotiations or neutral-site diplomacy—such as Switzerland or Turkey—could shift odds, but logistical, security, and geopolitical hurdles maintain the frontrunner's dominance.

Bayern trail by one goal on aggregate and must attack relentlessly at home, leveraging their five straight prior Champions League wins over PSG, but face defensive vulnerabilities exposed by PSG's clinical finishing from Khvicha Kvaratskhelia and others. Bayern's injury woes—Serge Gnabry (thigh, season-ending), Raphaël Guerreiro (hamstring), and Lennart Karl (out)—weaken depth, while PSG remain largely healthy despite Quentin Ndjantou's hamstring absence. Both sides' recent high-scoring form (PSG eight wins in nine, Bayern potent in knockouts) signals another open, goal-filled affair with no clear scoreline favorite.

markets.question: Yoon out of custody before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if President of South Korea Yoon Suk Yeol is released from custody by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If Yoon is released but remains under house arrest, the market will still resolve to "Yes".

If Yoon is released on parole, bond, or any other condition that results in them leaving state custody, the market will resolve to "Yes".

Transporting Yoon to another location of custody (e.g., a different prison, court, or hospital within the correctional system) will not suffice to resolve this market to "Yes".

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from government authorities or corrections departments; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Former South Korean President Yoon Suk-yeol remains in custody following his February 2026 conviction and life sentence for insurrection tied to the 2024 martial law declaration, with an appeals court on April 29 increasing his term to seven years for obstruction of arrest. These severe penalties, upheld amid multiple detention warrant extensions, reflect judicial consensus on charges from the political crisis that led to his impeachment and ouster by the Constitutional Court in April 2025. Absent successful higher appeals or extraordinary pardons—unlikely given the timeline to December 31, 2026—traders price a 92.5% implied probability of "No," emphasizing entrenched legal barriers over release scenarios.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in London be 19°C on May 7?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the London City Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 7 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the London City Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/gb/london/EGLC.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects high uncertainty in London's highest temperature on May 7, with 15°C, 17°C, and 16°C each around 22% implied probability, driven by the latest Met Office forecast indicating a 16°C maximum amid sunny intervals and 30-40% shower risk from a cooling midweek trend. Ensemble models from ECMWF, GFS, and UKMO show spread due to variable cloud cover and the timing of northerly air advection displacing earlier mild high-pressure influence, potentially capping peaks at 15°C with heavier showers or allowing 17°C under clearer skies. Light winds limit mixing, amplifying small differences in insolation. Updated forecasts twice daily from the Met Office could sharpen odds before resolution at official Heathrow observations.

markets.question: Will UBS or any of its underwriting affiliates serve as the lead underwriter in SpaceX’s initial public offering?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the bank that serves as the lead underwriter in the initial public offering of SpaceX.

If no IPO occurs by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, or SpaceX completes an initial public offering without a designated lead underwriter, this market will resolve to “Other.”

If multiple banks are identified as lead underwriters, this market will resolve according to the primary lead underwriter. If the hierarchy between them is unclear, this market will resolve once it is conclusively evident which bank is the primary lead underwriter, for example, through the order in which the banks are listed in the underwriting section of SpaceX’s final initial public offering prospectus, once released.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official disclosures from SpaceX. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket positions Morgan Stanley as the frontrunner at 49.5% implied probability to serve as lead bank for SpaceX's anticipated mega-IPO, driven by its longstanding relationship with Elon Musk—including underwriting Tesla's 2010 IPO—and recent positioning as an active bookrunner in the 21-bank "Project Apex" syndicate. Goldman Sachs (17.5%) and Bank of America (16.2%) trail closely, bolstered by Financial Times reporting in January 2026 naming them alongside JPMorgan and Morgan Stanley for senior roles, and a April 6 virtual banker meeting led by Musk and CFO Bret Johnsen outlining a $75 billion raise at up to $1.75 trillion valuation. With no single "lead left" designated—potentially alphabetical listing—uncertainty persists ahead of the late-May prospectus release and June roadshow.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the SC-02 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the SC-02 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: In this closely contested bantamweight bout on the UFC Vegas 117 main card, Timmy Cuamba (10-3, UFC 2-2) carries momentum from back-to-back wins, including a flying knee KO of Roberto Romero in April 2025 and unanimous decision over ChangHo Lee in November 2025, leveraging his 5'9" frame, 71" reach, and striker style (2.77 SLpM). Bernardo Sopaj (12-3, UFC 1-1), a high-volume orthodox striker (4.24 SLpM, 56% accuracy) with solid wrestling (2.53 TD avg.), returns after a 16-month layoff following his January 2025 unanimous decision rebound over Ricky Turcios, post-KO loss to Vinicius Oliveira. No recent injuries or cancellations reported ahead of May 16 at the APEX; stylistic clash favors Sopaj's pressure against Cuamba's distance management.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors world No. 1 Aryna Sabalenka at 31.5% implied probability for the 2026 Women's French Open, driven by her dominant 26-2 season record, multiple Madrid titles, and consistent deep clay runs amid the ongoing Rome draw where she eyes another WTA 1000 crown. Elena Rybakina's 13.6% surges post-Stuttgart WTA 500 win overtaking Sabalenka in Race to Riyadh standings, Coco Gauff holds 8.5% as defending Roland Garros champion with superior clay movement, while Mirra Andreeva (7%) and Marta Kostyuk (4.3%) impress via Madrid final runs.

markets.question: Will North and South Korea engage in direct talks by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, North and South Korea engage in direct talks, defined as an official meeting or communication between government representatives of both sides, conducted without a third-party relaying messages, by June 30, 2026 at 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise this market will resolve to “No”.

The talks may be in-person, by phone, or virtual, and must be publicly acknowledged by either government or reported by credible media. 

Routine military deconfliction, backchannel exchanges, or talks conducted entirely through another country or organization will not count. 

The resolutions source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

events.title: Will North and South Korea engage in direct talks by June 30?
events.description:

Otherwise this market will resolve to “No”.

The talks may be in-person, by phone, or virtual, and must be publicly acknowledged by either government or reported by credible media. 

Routine military deconfliction, backchannel exchanges, or talks conducted entirely through another country or organization will not count.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors no direct talks between North and South Korea by June 30, reflecting Pyongyang's entrenched rejection of inter-Korean dialogue amid its "two hostile states" policy toward Seoul. Despite South Korean President Lee Jae-myung's repeated calls for renewed negotiations since March and a brief thaw in early April—sparked by Seoul's apology for a civilian rally and reciprocal positive rhetoric—North Korea clarified on April 8 it seeks no clashes but explicitly rules out talks. No summits, working-level meetings, or official channels have been announced, with Kim Jong Un prioritizing military ties to Russia and signaling openness to separate U.S. diplomacy. Absent major de-escalation signals or scheduled diplomacy on the Korean Peninsula, traders see significant barriers to direct engagement within the timeline.

markets.question: Games Total: O/U 2.5
markets.description:
This market refers to the LoL Upper bracket quarterfinal 3 match between TLN Pirates and Esprit Shōnen in the LFL Playoffs, initially scheduled for May 6 at 12:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to "Over" if TLN Pirates and Esprit Shōnen play 3 or more games in this series.

If fewer than 3 games are played, this market will resolve to "Under".

Games won by forfeit, disqualification, walkover, or default are counted towards the total, provided that the match is completed.

If the match is canceled (not played at all for any reason), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's match forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends due to the clinching game being forfeited this will count as a completed match.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

events.title: LoL: TLN Pirates vs Esprit Shōnen (BO3) - LFL Playoffs
events.description:
This market refers to the LoL Upper bracket quarterfinal 3 match between TLN Pirates and Esprit Shōnen in the LFL Playoffs, initially scheduled for May 6 at 12:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to "TLN Pirates" if TLN Pirates win the match against Esprit Shōnen.

This market will resolve to "Esprit Shōnen" if Esprit Shōnen win the match against TLN Pirates.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: TLN Pirates, fresh off a dominant 7-2 regular season finish as the No. 2 seed in LFL 2026 Spring, face No. 7 Esprit Shōnen in the upper bracket quarterfinals BO3 on May 6 under fearless draft rules. Pirates capped the round-robin with a 1-0 win over Shōnen on May 1, extending their head-to-head edge amid just two losses all split to teams like Vitality.Bee and ZYB Esport. Shōnen's 3-6 record reflects inconsistency despite a stable roster featuring xKenzuke mid and Mxe support, while Pirates' core of Spooder top, Stefan jungle, and Toffe mid drives trader consensus toward their seeding advantage in this double-elimination playoff path to EMEA Masters qualification. No roster changes or substitutes reported post-regular season.

markets.question: Will New Slovenia – Christian Democrats (NSi) be part of the next Government of Slovenia?
markets.description:
Parliamentary elections are scheduled to be held in Slovenia on March 22, 2026.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed political party is included in the first Slovenian government formed after the 2026 Slovenian parliamentary elections. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market may resolve once the first government is officially confirmed following a successful confirmation vote in the Slovenian National Assembly (Državni zbor), with ministers sworn in under non-caretaker circumstances.

A party will only be considered part of the coalition if it signs the coalition agreement and provides at least one cabinet minister. Parties that merely support the government (e.g., through confidence-and-supply arrangements, parliamentary support agreements, or similar) without signing the coalition agreement and holding a cabinet post will not qualify.

If no government is formed, or the results are not known definitively by January 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve based on a consensus of credible reporting. In case of ambiguity, this market will resolve based on official information from the Government of Slovenia.

eventMetadata.context_description: Following Slovenia's proportional representation parliamentary elections on March 22, 2026, no party achieved a majority in the 90-seat National Assembly, requiring 46 seats for a government. Incumbent Prime Minister Robert Golob's centre-left Freedom Movement (GS) took 29 seats, just ahead of Janez Janša's right-wing Slovenian Democratic Party (SDS) with 28, while the NSi-SLS-Fokus centre-right bloc secured 9, and smaller parties like Democrats (6 seats), Social Democrats (6), Levica-Vesna (5), and Resni.ca (5) hold kingmaker roles. Golob's broad coalition bid collapsed on April 20 amid rejections from centre-right groups, prompting President Nataša Pirc Musar on April 25 to task lawmakers with nominating a viable prime minister candidate via parliamentary votes within 14 days. Recent momentum favors a centre-right coalition led by SDS, with proposals to trim ministries signaling advanced talks; failure could trigger snap elections.

markets.question: Will Mistral have the #2 AI model at the end of June 2026 (Style Control On)?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the company that owns the model that has the second-highest arena rank (Style Control On) based on the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard (https://lmarena.ai/) when the table under the "Leaderboard" tab is checked on June 30, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

Results from the "Rank" column under the "Text Arena | Overall" Leaderboard tab at https://lmarena.ai/leaderboard/text with style control on will be used to resolve this market.

Models will be ordered primarily by their leaderboard rank at the market’s check time. If two or more models are tied on rank, they will be ordered by their Arena score, including any underlying, unrounded, granular values reflected in the data below the leaderboard. If a tie remains, alphabetical order of company names as listed in this market group will be used as a final tiebreaker (e.g., if the two models are tied by exact arena score, “Google” would be ranked ahead of “xAI”). This market will resolve based on the company that occupies second place under this ranking system.

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source is unavailable at check time, this market will remain open until the leaderboard comes back online and will resolve based on the first check after it becomes available. If it becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve based on another resolution source.

markets.question: Decibel FDV above $200M one day after launch?

The FDV will be determined using the total token supply multiplied by the token price.

"1 day after launch" is defined as 4:00 PM ET on the calendar day following launch. The resolution source for this market is the most liquid price source available. If Decibel (https://x.com/DecibelTrade) doesn't launch a token by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

markets.question: Will Adam Schiff be arrested before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed individual is arrested by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A qualifying arrest/detention includes:
* Being taken into physical custody by law enforcement authorities (including federal, state, local, military, or international law enforcement)
* Voluntarily surrendering to law enforcement authorities in response to an arrest warrant
* Being formally booked or processed following detention
* Being handcuffed by a state authority and taken to a police station
* Surrendering at a police station or courthouse with their attorney
* Being placed under house arrest or electronic monitoring

The following scenarios will NOT qualify as an arrest/detention
* An arrest warrant being issued but not executed
* Being briefly detained but not arrested (e.g. such as in the South Korean judicial system when an individual is awaiting a judge’s decision on whether to grant a detention warrant)
* Being questioned or interviewed without arrest
* Being named in an indictment without arrest

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the arresting government and law enforcement, however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Khamenei # posts May 1 - May 8, 2026?
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Khamenei (@khamenei_ir), posts on X between May 1, 12:00 PM ET and May 8, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies which are recorded on the main feed will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

The resolution source for this market is the "Post Counter" figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices a low volume of posts from the official @khamenei_ir X account—archival statements from the late Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei—at under 5 (56.5%) or 5-9 (41.5%) during May 1-8, driven by its prolonged silence since the April 13 post amid the leadership transition following his February 28 martyrdom in US-Israeli strikes. Recent US intelligence reports detailing severe injuries to successor Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei, including limited official access, have fueled expectations of subdued activity, contrasting the new @MKhamenei_ir account's May 1 cluster of nine posts marking Workers' and Teachers' Days. Earlier April markets resolved around 10-14 posts, but three weeks without updates signal trader caution on resumption.

markets.question: Will Bitcoin dip to $50,000 by December 31, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will immediately resolve to "Yes" if any Binance 1 minute candle for Bitcoin (BTC/USDT) between November 24, 2025, 14:00 and December 31, 2026, 23:59 in the ET timezone has a final "Low" price equal to or lower than the price specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT "Low" prices available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT, with the chart settings on "1m" for one-minute candles selected on the top bar.

Please note that the outcome of this market depends solely on the price data from the Binance BTC/USDT trading pair. Prices from other exchanges, different trading pairs, or spot markets will not be considered for the resolution of this market.

markets.question: Will the Democratic Party win the NY-21 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the NY-21 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors Republicans at 60% to retain the open NY-21 House seat—a Solid Republican district with an R+10 lean—reflecting historical dominance where former Rep. Elise Stefanik won by wide margins before pivoting to the 2026 gubernatorial race. Recent GOP primary developments, including former President Trump's April 21 endorsement of Anthony Constantino over locally backed Assemblyman Robert Smullen amid internal polls showing a tight contest (Smullen leading 43%-33% in late April), have kept focus on nominee strength ahead of the June 23 primaries. Democrats' fragmented primary field lacks a clear frontrunner despite Gov. Kathy Hochul's push to target the seat, limiting their path in this battleground-resistant district despite statewide generic ballot edges.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Manila be 35°C on May 7?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Ninoy Aquino International Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 7 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Ninoy Aquino International Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/ph/manila/RPLL.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: PAGASA's latest 5-day outlook for Metro Manila's Port Area forecasts a 36°C high on May 7 amid partly cloudy skies with isolated rainshowers or thunderstorms driven by easterlies, anchoring trader sentiment with 28% implied probability on that outcome and near-parity for 37°C, 35°C, and 34°C. This tight clustering reflects inherent short-range forecast uncertainty from variable afternoon sea breeze timing, potential convective cloud development suppressing peaks by 1-3°C, and urban heat island amplification in Manila. An ongoing El Niño alert signals drier conditions boosting heat potential above May's 34°C historical average, though model ensembles diverge slightly. Watch PAGASA's daily updates for refined guidance ahead of resolution based on official Quezon City station observations.

markets.question: Will Donald Trump publicly insult someone on May 16, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Donald Trump makes any public statement in which he insults, mocks, or attacks any non-fictional individual personally or professionally in a clearly negative manner on the specified date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No". 

This includes calling the individual weak, stupid, disloyal, a failure, using an insulting nickname, using other derogatory language, or using the negative form of a positive trait in a derogatory personal way (e.g., “He/She isn’t smart”). Negative forms used in reference to the individual's professional actions, policies, or decisions (e.g., “He/She isn’t being smart about this policy”) will not count. Policy disagreements stated without disparaging language will not count.

A direct reference will qualify even if the individual is not named, so long as it is reasonably clear from context that they are the subject.

Any written, verbal, or recorded public statement by Trump qualifies.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: President Donald Trump's frequent public insults via Truth Social remain a hallmark of his communication style, particularly amid ongoing U.S.-Israeli military actions against Iran and domestic criticism of the war. In the past month, he unleashed expletive-filled rants at Iranian leaders on April 5, bashed conservative media figures including Tucker Carlson on April 9 for opposing escalation, and attacked Pope Leo XIV on April 13 over perceived interference. Following Carlson's April 25 critique, anticipation of retaliation peaked. No verified insults in the last 48 hours, but simmering tensions with critics, allies, and adversaries signal high trader consensus for recurrence, with traders monitoring daily posts, rallies, or diplomatic developments that could prompt responses before market resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors Connor McDavid at 54.4% implied probability for the Hart Memorial Trophy after he paced the NHL with 138 points (48 goals, 90 assists) in 82 games, capping the 2025-26 regular season with a late surge that propelled the Edmonton Oilers into playoff contention despite a midseason slump. Nikita Kucherov sits at 31.5% following his 130 points (44G, 86A) in just 76 games, driving the Tampa Bay Lightning's strong Eastern Conference finish with elite power-play production and a +43 plus/minus. Nathan MacKinnon's 15.0% reflects his league-leading 53 goals and +57 rating that powered Colorado's top Western seed, though trailing in total points. Finalists announcement looms May 8 amid ongoing Stanley Cup Playoffs, but regular-season impact dominates voting.

markets.question: Will "Pinocchio" be said on ICEMAN?
markets.description:
Drake is expected to release his new album "ICEMAN" in 2026.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed term is mentioned by anyone on the specified transcript for any track on the officially released full "ICEMAN" album. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

Officially released means that Iceman is officially available for download or streaming (not including live events) by the resolution date. Any Drake album officially confirmed to be the Iceman project will count, regardless of potential name changes.

Samples or clips of audio where people are speaking or singing which are included in the album will qualify toward this market's resolution so long as it is included in the specified transcript. AI-generated audio will count toward this market's resolution so long as it is included in the specified transcript.

In the case a term is censored on a relevant transcript, audio may be consulted to verify that a qualifying mention occurred.

Misspellings or iterations of the listed term, including all grammatical or slang forms, or misspellings with extra, missing, or incorrect letters (ex: helloooooooo or heoll, for ‘hello’), will not count toward a “Yes” resolution, regardless of context or intent.

Any usage of the term, regardless of context, will count toward the resolution of this market.

Plural and possessive forms of the listed term will count toward the resolution of this market regardless of context; however, other forms will NOT count.

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g. joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

If this market requires a specified number of mentions of a person’s first or last name, a full-name mention will count as one mention (e.g., if a market is about “Joe / Biden 5+ times,” a mention of “Joe Biden” will count once).

This market will resolve according to the initial release of the full album on a qualifying streaming platform. Releases consisting only of individual songs or remixes released later will not qualify.

The resolution source for this market will be the audio transcripts for this album provided by the listed streaming platforms in the following order of priority: Spotify, Apple Music, YouTube Music, Amazon Music, Tidal (e.g., Spotify will be used unless it does not provide a necessary transcript, then Apple Music will be used, etc.).

If no such album is released in full by by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to based on what has been released up to that point. If the album is not released on any of the listed platforms by the specified end date, this market will resolve to "No".

Streamer Adin Ross, privy to insider details, recently teased features on stream while shutting down rumored collabs like Morgan Wallen, heightening buzz without spoilers. Post-release Spotify transcripts will dictate resolutions, with first-week Billboard 200 performance and streaming surges as key post-drop catalysts amid reported UMG promotion hurdles.

markets.question: Will Katie Porter win the California Governor Election in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to according to the candidate who wins the 2026 California gubernatorial election currently scheduled for November 3, 2026.

If the results of the election are not confirmed by July 31, 2027, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market is the Associated Press, Fox News, and NBC. This market will resolve once all three sources call the race for the same candidate. If all three sources haven’t called the race in this state for the same candidate, this market will resolve based on official certification.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices Xavier Becerra slightly ahead of Tom Steyer in the 2026 California gubernatorial race, reflecting Becerra's late-April surge in polls like Gudelunas (24%) and a May tie with Steve Hilton, driven by his high name recognition, +33 net favorability, and experience as former Attorney General and HHS Secretary. Steyer holds close via massive self-funding—poised for the most expensive campaign—and progressive endorsements like Our Revolution, despite scrutiny over past coal investments. The top-two primary on June 2 keeps the race tight amid a splintered Democratic field post-Swalwell and Yee exits, risking Republican advancement by Hilton or Riverside Sheriff Chad Bianco if votes fragment further; upcoming debates and final polls could consolidate support and create separation.

Begich's edge stems from Democratic unity, family name recognition, and broad voter appeal, while Wilson's conservative activism and anti-RCV stance compete in a crowded Republican field splitting support among Anchorage Mayor David Bronson, Lt. Gov. Nancy Dahlstrom, Sen. Click Bishop, and AG Treg Taylor. Consolidation could arise from GOP endorsements, dropouts before the June 1 filing deadline, fundraising momentum, or forums clarifying stances on Permanent Fund Dividend payouts and resource development ahead of the August 18 primary.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus crowns Jannik Sinner the clear favorite at 50.5% implied probability for the 2026 Men’s Wimbledon title, propelled by his reign as year-end world No. 1 with a dominant 73-6 record in 2024, including his maiden grass-court triumph at Halle over Hubert Hurkacz. Carlos Alcaraz trails at 18.5% as the two-time defending champion on the All England Club lawns, leveraging back-to-back Grand Slam wins there despite a clay-heavy focus. Novak Djokovic's 9.8% reflects lingering knee surgery recovery from mid-2024 and age-related concerns despite seven prior titles, while Alexander Zverev (3.5%) and British hopeful Jack Draper (2.3%) gain traction from recent ATP form and surface suitability. No major developments in the past 30 days have shifted sentiment, with markets pricing grass history, rankings, and matchup edges.

markets.question: Will Doja Cat have a #1 song on the Billboard Hot 100 in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if any song by the listed artist is the number 1 song in any official weekly Billboard Hot 100 songs chart released for a data collection reference period entirely between January 1 and December 31, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Any song released with the listed artist as a primary artist will count, including songs released before 2026. If a song is released jointly by multiple artists credited equally as primary artists, it will count for all of them. Features, writing credits, production credits, or other forms of non-primary contributions to a song will not count. Artist attribution will be determined solely by the artists displayed by Billboard on the relevant chart.

Billboard updates its Hot 100 songs chart each Tuesday (with adjusted release schedules on some holiday weeks), reflecting data collected in the previous week (Friday-Thursday). Each Billboard chart is then titled “Week of (date of the upcoming Saturday)”. The first Billboard Hot 100 song chart relevant to this market will be the chart titled “Week of January 17, 2026” for a data collection reference period of January 2-8, 2026. The final Billboard Hot 100 song chart relevant to this market will be the Chart titled “Week of January 9, 2027” for a data collection reference period of December 25-31, 2026.

Updates or revisions made within this market’s timeframe to the #1-ranked song on previously published Billboard Hot 100 charts will be considered; however, such updates will not disqualify a previously published Billboard Hot 100 #1 song from counting. If a Billboard Hot 100 #1 song is revised after initial publication, both the originally published #1 song and the revised #1 song will count for this market. Updates or revisions made after the release of the final Billboard Hot 100 song chart relevant to this market will not be considered.

The resolution source for this market will be the weekly Billboard Hot 100 songs charts published each week at https://www.billboard.com/charts/hot-100/ and through other official Billboard channels.

eventMetadata.context_description: Ella Langley's "Choosin' Texas" surged back to Billboard Hot 100 No. 1 for an eighth non-consecutive week on the May 9 chart, highlighting sustained country-pop crossover streaming dominance after Olivia Rodrigo's "Drop Dead" notched a one-week reign the prior frame. Eight artists have secured 2026 #1s to date—Mariah Carey, Taylor Swift (two songs), Bruno Mars, Harry Styles, Bad Bunny, BTS, Ella Langley, and Rodrigo—leaving trader sentiment focused on proven hitmakers like Drake ahead of his Iceman album drop, Ariana Grande's ongoing campaign, and Playboi Carti's hype-driven singles. Weekly chart Tuesdays track streaming, airplay, and sales trajectories, with Post Malone and Morgan Wallen collabs signaling potential summer disruptors amid fierce genre competition.

markets.question: Spread: Manchester City FC (-1.5)
markets.description:
In the upcoming Premier League game, scheduled for March 21 at 11:00 AM ET:

This market will resolve to "Manchester City FC" if Manchester City FC win the game by 2 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Crystal Palace FC".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on premierleague.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Manchester City FC vs. Crystal Palace FC - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Premier League game, scheduled for March 21 at 11:00 AM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Manchester City's home Premier League clash with Crystal Palace, originally set for March 21 at Etihad Stadium, was postponed due to City's participation in the Carabao Cup final against Arsenal the following day, with no rescheduled date confirmed yet—likely in late April or May midweeks. Sitting second in the table with 61 points from 30 matches, nine behind leaders Arsenal, City view this game in hand as pivotal for their title push amid a tight top-four race including Manchester United and Aston Villa. Palace, mid-table battlers, face defensive injury woes with Daniel Munoz, Cheick Doucoure, and Jean Mateta sidelined long-term, while City manage knocks to Nico O'Reilly and Josko Gvardiol alongside Mateo Kovacic's Achilles absence. Recent City form shows rotation depth, but home advantage and superior goal difference favor them upon rescheduling.

markets.question: Elon Bull Run Parlay
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if all of the following conditions are met between January 1 and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET:

- Elon Musk trillionaire
- Another Elon baby
- 9+ SpaceX Starship launches reach space

markets.question: Will Coinbase Global, Inc. (COIN) hit (HIGH) $215 in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during May 2026, any 1-minute candle for Coinbase Global, Inc. (COIN) has a final "High" price equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange on which the listed security trades (typically 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM ET) will be considered. Prices occurring during pre-market or after-hours trading will not qualify.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Pyth after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Coinbase Global, Inc. (COIN) "High" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.COIN%2FUSD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter. Any timestamp within the listed market time frame may be used to view the relevant candle data (e.g., https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.COIN%2FUSD?t=1773432000)

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily high price published by the primary exchange on which the listed security trades will be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

markets.question: Will Kim Jong Un be the next leader out before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the first individual who ceases to occupy their listed office.

An announcement of a resignation/removal, or a scheduled departure from office due to the outcome of an election, will not alone qualify.

Only permanent removal from office will qualify for resolution. Temporary removals, such as impeachment suspensions (e.g., Yoon Suk Yeol's recent impeachment), temporary invocation of the 25th Amendment, or any similar provisional transfers of power, will not count.

Additionally, if an individual continues in a caretaker or interim role (e.g., Gabriel Attal remaining as caretaker Prime Minister of France), they will not be considered to have ceased occupying the office for the purposes of this market.

If this criterion has not been met for any of the listed individuals by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “None before 2027”. No additional individuals will be added to this market after its creation.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

markets.question: Will Trump say "Hottest" during events with Xi Jinping?
markets.description:
Donald Trump is scheduled to meet with Xi Jinping from May 14 to May 15, 2026 (https://www.cnn.com/2026/05/04/china/china-us-talks-iran-intl-hnk). 

This market will resolve to "Yes" if Donald Trump says the listed term during his appearance at events featuring Xi Jinping from May 14 through May 15, 2026. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

Plural and possessive forms of the listed term will count toward the resolution of this market regardless of context; however, other forms will NOT count.

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g. joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

If this market requires a specified number of mentions of a person’s first or last name, a full-name mention will count as one mention (e.g., if a market is about “Joe / Biden 5+ times,” a mention of “Joe Biden” will count once).

AI-generated audio or video will not count toward this market's resolution.

This market is explicitly about events featuring both Donald Trump and Xi Jinping from May 14 through May 15, 2026. All such events featuring both on these dates count. Speeches, events, or comments which feature one named individual but not the other, will not qualify toward this market's resolution. If the events contain a Q&A, it will count toward the resolution of this market.

If this visit is definitively cancelled, or otherwise is not aired by May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source will be video of the events. Only remarks which are broadcast or streamed live will count toward this market's resolution.

Today's observed high of 18°C—cooler than the early-May climatological average of 21°C due to lingering cloud cover—has introduced caution, but model ensembles anticipate enhanced solar heating from a stabilizing high-pressure ridge, with potential for 1-2°C variability. Light breezes may limit peaks, while genuine uncertainty persists in precise boundary-layer mixing. KMA's evening update could shift odds before resolution via official station measurements.

eventMetadata.context_description: Atlético Mineiro's superior Serie A quality and 2-1 group stage victory over Juventud three weeks ago in Belo Horizonte drive their 72.5% implied probability as favorites in this pivotal Copa Sudamericana Group B clash at Estadio Centenario. Despite key absences like suspended defender Preciado and injuries to Cissé and others post-Hulk's departure, Mineiro's attacking depth with Scarpa, Dudu, and Reinier, bolstered by a confidence-boosting 3-1 derby win over Cruzeiro last weekend, outweighs Juventud's home edge and mixed Primera División form (recent 4-0 rout of Puerto Cabello but draws elsewhere). Both trail leader Cienciano on similar points, elevating stakes, yet Juventud's slim 4.5% upset chance reflects their underdog status, with draw at 22.5% viable in a tight contest.

markets.question: Will EUR/USD hit 1.35 (High) in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Investing.com high price (“H”) for any EUR/USD hourly candle for an hour on or before the listed end date (ET) is equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Data for a given candle will be considered finalized once the next candle appears on the specified graph. The last trading day of a given week will be considered finalized once the market closes on that day, typically at 5 PM ET on Friday.

This market will resolve as soon as any finalized EUR/USD hourly candle high price is equal to or above the listed price, or once the final hourly candle in the specified period is finalized. A candle starting at 11:00 PM ET on a given date will be considered to be on that date.

This market’s resolution will be based solely on information from the “H” figure located at the top of the EUR/USD Streaming Chart on Investing.com for the specified currency pair (e.g., https://www.investing.com/currencies/eur-usd-chart).

eventMetadata.context_description: EUR/USD hovers near 1.1720 as of May 5, 2026, reflecting persistent Fed-ECB policy divergence, with the Federal Reserve holding rates around 4.00-4.75% amid resilient U.S. growth while the ECB has cut to approximately 2.25% deposit rate following earlier easing. Eurozone April inflation accelerated to 3%, up from March's 2.6%, fueling speculation of a potential June ECB hike that drove the pair's rally from March lows near 1.1435, despite Q1 GDP expanding just 0.1% and unemployment steady at 6.2%. Traders price in modest euro appreciation via ECB professional forecasters' Q2 median of 1.18, eyeing FOMC June 16-17 and ECB July 22-23 meetings, plus U.S. nonfarm payrolls and Eurozone PMIs as key catalysts for rate path repricing through year-end.

markets.question: Will the Democratic Party win the NY-13 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the NY-13 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: New York's 13th Congressional District, encompassing heavily Democratic Upper Manhattan neighborhoods with high renter populations, overwhelmingly favors Democrats, reflected in trader consensus pricing the Democratic Party at 93.5% to win the November 3, 2026, general election. Incumbent Rep. Adriano Espaillat, first elected in 2017 and reelected decisively in 2024, bolsters this position amid a lack of credible Republican challengers, as no major GOP candidate has emerged. Recent developments center on the June 23 Democratic primary, where four challengers—including Darializa Avila Chevalier, who polls competitively in internals—filed against Espaillat, but victory by any Democrat secures the seat per historical patterns in this solid D-rated district. Scenarios challenging this include a primary upset producing an unelectable nominee, late GOP recruitment of a high-profile contender, or unforeseen scandals, though barriers remain high given the partisan lean.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Seoul be 19°C on May 7?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Incheon Intl Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 7 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Incheon Intl Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/kr/incheon/RKSI.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Latest Korea Meteorological Administration (KMA) forecasts project a maximum temperature near 18°C in the Seoul-Incheon area on May 7, driving trader consensus with 29.5% implied probability on 18°C, closely trailed by 17°C (28.5%) and 16°C (21.5%). This clustering reflects model consensus on partly cloudy conditions with afternoon rain risks from a passing weak front, capping daytime heating despite light southwest winds aiding mild advection; historical early-May highs average 21–22°C under clearer skies. Differentiating factors include cloud cover variability—greater persistence favors 16–17°C, while earlier clearing or reduced precipitation could yield 18–19°C—and minor station-specific differences between Seoul's urban core and nearby Incheon readings. KMA short-range updates, issued multiple times daily, will refine this ahead of resolution based on official Seoul observations.

events.title: Cádiz CF vs. RC Deportivo La Coruña - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the La Liga 2 game, scheduled for May 8 at 2:30 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: RC Deportivo La Coruña, entrenched in second place in LaLiga Hypermotion standings with 64 points from a strong run of wins and draws—including a 3-1 victory over Mirandés last weekend and a 1-1 draw at Burgos on April 25—enter Jornada 39 as promotion contenders against relegation-battling Cádiz CF, mired in 18th on 38 points. Cádiz's winless streak over five matches features heavy losses like 3-0 to Sporting Gijón and 3-0 at Real Valladolid, exacerbated by injuries to key attackers Javi Ontiveros (groin), Suso (muscle), and Bojan Kovacevic (knee). The earlier season 2-2 draw at Riazor highlights Cádiz's home fightback potential, but Deportivo's away form and table position drive trader sentiment toward the visitors.

eventMetadata.context_description: Aubry Bracco commands 89.5% implied probability as Survivor 50's frontrunner, her strategic mastery—honed as Kaôh Rōng runner-up and Game Changers contender—driving trader consensus through high-volume bets on Polymarket. Recent Episode 10's revived auction twist, amplified by MrBeast's disruptive Super Beware advantage, saw her sidestep chaos while Stephenie LaGrossa Kendrick earned the boot, thinning threats and bolstering Aubry's alliances in the final nine. Competitors like Joe Hunter (3.9%) and Cirie Fields (2.6%) trail amid fan-favorite dynamics and immunity scrambles, but endgame jury management remains unpredictable ahead of the live finale. Traders' skin-in-the-game bets signal overwhelming momentum for her Sole Survivor campaign.
